{{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka, Osaka|ja|豊中市}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka, Osaka|ja|豊中市}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka, Osaka|ja|豊中市}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[Category:東田大塚古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:東田大塚古墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka, Osaka|ja|豊中市}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka, Osaka|ja|豊中市}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka, Osaka|ja|豊中市}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station (Osaka)|ja|曽根駅}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sekkan|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == 交通アクセス == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|ja|岡町駅}} * '''National Route 176''' [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:01-17]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] [[category:Ōtsuka Kofun (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]
