Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.323697|N|136.898208|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537961]] Wikidata name: Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Toyonaka|Toyonaka, Osaka]], in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''[[:en:Okamachi Station|Okamachi Station]] on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''[[:en:Sone Station|Sone Station (Osaka)]]'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - [[:en:Okamachi Station|Okamachi Station]] * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]] * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - [[:en:Okamachi Station|Okamachi Station]] * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.323697|N|136.898208|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537961]] Wikidata name: Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka, Osaka}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station|en|Sone Station|WD=Q7560924|lt=Sone Station (Osaka)}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}} * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka, Osaka}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station|en|Sone Station|WD=Q7560924|lt=Sone Station (Osaka)}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}} * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.323697|N|136.898208|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}} {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka, Osaka}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station|en|Sone Station|WD=Q7560924|lt=Sone Station (Osaka)}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}} * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}} * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station}} * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|1=Toyonaka}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.323697|N|136.898208|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}}} {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka, Osaka|1=Toyonaka}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station|1=Okamachi Station}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station|en|Sone Station|WD=Q7560924|lt=Sone Station (Osaka)|1=Sone Station}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station|1=Okamachi Station}} * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)|version=103654976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?sourceid=navclient&hl=ja&ie=UTF-8&q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E5%B2%A1%E7%94%BA%E5%8C%971-6 豊中市岡町北1-6]] [[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?q=%E8%B1%8A%E4%B8%AD%E5%B8%82%E4%B8%AD%E6%A1%9C%E5%A1%9A4-15 豊中市中桜塚4-15]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)}} [[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11537961}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Toyonaka, Osaka]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sakurazuka Kofun Group (Toyonaka)]][[en:Sakurazuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|1=Toyonaka}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.323697|N|136.898208|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}}} {{No footnotes|date=October 2017}} [[File:桜塚古墳群分布図.jpg|thumb|350x350px|Map of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group
{{ill|Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudo Kōkū Shashin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}]]'''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' (桜塚古墳群) is a {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|Toyonaka|WD=Q467479|lt=Toyonaka, Osaka|1=Toyonaka}}, in the areas of Okamachi, Nakasakurazuka, and Minamisakurazuka, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Five of the kofun are designated as '''National Historic Sites'''. The group is situated on the '''Toyonaka Plateau''', at an elevation of approximately '''20-25 meters''', forming a '''low terrace'''. It extends '''1.2 kilometers east-west''' and '''1 kilometer north-south''', centering around '''{{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station|1=Okamachi Station}} on the Hankyu Takarazuka Line'''. == Overview == The kofun in this group were built in two main phases: - The '''late early Kofun period (4th century)''' - The '''late middle to early late Kofun period (6th century)''' The first known investigations took place in '''1874, 1879, and 1881''', with large-scale surveys producing maps such as: * '''"Ichinichi Sansha Roku Fun Zenzu"''' (壱日三捨六墳全図) * '''"Sanjuurokufun Shozai Souzu"''' (三十六墳所在総図) * '''"Shofun Ryakuzu Menshoage"''' (諸墳略図面書上) During the '''1930s''', excavation of '''Kitsunezuka Kofun''', '''North Tenbinzuka Kofun''', and '''South Tenbinzuka Kofun''' revealed that this was the '''largest kofun group in the Hokusettsu region'''. The group originally contained '''44 kofun''', including '''8 additional tombs (Sakurazuka Tombs No. 37–44)''' discovered in recent investigations. However, '''39 kofun were destroyed''' due to residential development, and only '''five remain''' today: * '''Ōishizuka Kofun''' (大石塚古墳) * '''Koishizuka Kofun''' (小石塚古墳) * '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) * '''Oshishizuka Kofun''' (御獅子塚古墳) * '''Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun''' (南天平塚古墳) The Sakurazuka Kofun Group is divided into '''four sub-groups''': * '''West Group''': Centered around Ōishizuka and Koishizuka Kofun. * '''Central Group''': Includes '''Kōjinzuka Kofun''' and '''Sakurazuka Kofun'''. * '''East Group''': Centered around '''Ōtsuka and Oshishizuka Kofun'''. * '''South Group''': Newly discovered kofun northeast of '''{{ill|Sone Station|en|Sone Station|WD=Q7560924|lt=Sone Station (Osaka)|1=Sone Station}}'''. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids. Important artifacts discovered in this kofun group include: - A '''gilded phoenix openwork belt fitting''' from Kōjinzuka Kofun - A '''fine-line decorated beast-belt mirror''' from an unidentified tomb == 古墳群 == === Ōishizuka Kofun === Ōishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' located at the '''western edge''' of the Sakurazuka Kofun Group. Its main axis aligns '''north-south''' with Koishizuka Kofun, suggesting a planned construction. A '''1979 survey''' revealed that it is the '''largest kofun in the group''', measuring '''80+ meters in length''' and '''48 meters in diameter at the rear'''. The mound was '''three-tiered''' with '''river stones as [[fukiishi]]''' and '''gravel spread on each terrace'''. The name "Ishizuka" (stone mound) likely derives from these features. Excavations also found '''cylindrical and morning-glory-shaped haniwa''', now displayed at the '''Toyonaka Traditional Performing Arts Museum'''. This kofun was part of '''Harada Shrine's domain''' and was historically called '''"Goizuka" (御位塚) Kofun'''. It was '''designated as a National Historic Site in 1956'''. * '''Type''': Keyhole-shaped kofun * '''Dimensions''': 87.2m x 45.4m, height 5.0m * '''Location''': === Koishizuka Kofun === [[File:大石塚小石塚古墳.JPG|thumb|250x250px|Koishizuka Kofun]] Located '''north of Ōishizuka Kofun''', Koishizuka Kofun is a '''south-facing keyhole-shaped kofun''' with a '''49-meter total length'''. A '''1979 excavation''' revealed: * The '''rear mound was two-tiered''', while the '''front was one-tiered'''. * A '''moat existed only on the east side'''. * '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} ('''{{ill|Cylindrical Haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|lt='''cylindrical'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}''', jar, and''' {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|lt='''morning-glory types'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}''') were present but displaced'''. * A '''{{ill|clay coffin chamber|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}''' was identified, likely holding a {{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|lt='''split-log wooden coffin'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|qq=}} oriented north-south. * '''Type''': {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * '''Dimensions''': 49.0m x 29.0m, height 3.6m * '''Location''': === Ōtsuka Kofun === [[File:Toyonaka Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun]] Ōtsuka Kofun represents the '''East Group''' and is '''the second largest kofun''' in the group. A '''1930s land development project''' removed part of the '''northern and western embankments'''. By the 1980s, artifacts had begun to surface, leading to '''emergency excavation in 1983'''. Findings revealed: * '''A three-tiered round mound''' * '''A 12-13m wide moat''', making its total diameter '''80m''' * '''Three burial chambers aligned north-south''' * '''Iron armor, swords, and shields''' were recovered, now National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} * '''Type''': {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * '''Dimensions''': 56.0m x 46.9m, height 18.0m * '''Location''': == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * '''Sakurazuka Kofun Group''' ** '''1956''': Ōishizuka & Koishizuka Kofun designated. ** '''1987''': Ōtsuka, Oshishizuka, and Minami Tenbinzuka Kofun added. ** '''1998''': Part of Koishizuka Kofun expanded. == 桜塚の地名の由来 == The '''Sakurazuka''' name derives from '''Sakurazuka Kofun''', though some claim it originally referred to '''Kōjinzuka Kofun'''. The latter had '''a large cherry tree''' on its summit, which was discovered '''during demolition in 1922'''. == Access == * '''Hankyu Takarazuka Line''' - {{ill|Okamachi Station|en|Okamachi Station|WD=Q3970333|lt=Okamachi Station|1=Okamachi Station}} * '''National Route 176''' == External Links == * [http://www.city.toyonaka.osaka.jp/jinken_gakushu/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/kinenbutsu/shiseki/shiseki001.html Sakurazuka Kofun Group - Toyonaka City] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. One '''notable feature''' of the East Group is the use of '''kasu-gai (鎹, clamps)''' to secure coffin lids.
